title: Using Github to create a eNotebook
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6a3hf","text":"Instructions to create a Github Account and your first repository.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Browse to the Github website https://github.com/
Click on the Sign-up button on the right corner of the screen.
Follow the instructions to create your account. To the Qestion 1, answer that you are working alone and that you are a student.
In the recommended pricing plan, select "Continue for free". This will lead you to this home interface:
Now that you have an account, let's create the repository you'll need for this CURE class. Click on the green button on the left corner "Create repository"
First, create the name of the repository to be "CURE2022_{YOUR INITIALS AND NAME}" Then fill the description to be "BAT 102 - CURE class repository 2022" Leave the repository as "Public"
Click the tick box "Add a README.md" and select the licence as "MIT license"
Now click the green button "Create repository". Congratulations, you have created your first repository! Your interface should now look like this:
We will use the wiki function of the Github repository as a eNotebook. On the top of the screen, click on the "Wiki" tab, and create the first page. You should see this interface:

You can now fill your first entry of your lab Notebook. Paste the following text: # Welcome to the my eNotebook! This is an electronic Notebook for the "Data Science Heroes" Research Project - from the CURE class BAT102. More information about the class and the research project is available at https://hurwitzlab.shinyapps.io/DS_Hero Note that in Markdown, the "#" sign signals a header. You can see a preview of your page by clicking on the tab "Preview". You can now click on the button "Create page" to save the first entry of your Notebook!
You are now done with creating your notebook and your first entry. If you notice a mistake or want to correct something , you can modify the page you created using the button "edit".
creator: Alise J Ponsero
